notes: He should not "multiply horses" (vs. 16). In the ancient Middle East, horses were used primarily in warfare. One Bible scholar believed this use was forbidden "lest the people might depend on a well-appointed cavalry as a means of security, and so cease from trusting in the strength and protection of God. And … that they might not be tempted to extend their dominion by means of cavalry, and so get scattered among the surrounding idolatrous nations, and thus cease, in process of time, to be that
